zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathew ap Gwalter於大約1235年出生在英國威爾斯布洛可爾維斯頓時，他的父親Gwgon ap Bleddyn, Arglwydd of Cas wis141歲，母親Gwenllian verch Philip142歲。他於1265年與Margared Gwys在英國威爾斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathew ap Gwalter于大约1235年出生在英国威尔斯布洛可爾維斯頓时，他的父亲Gwgon ap Bleddyn, Arglwydd of Cas wis141岁，母亲Gwenllian verch Philip142岁。他于1265年与Margared Gwys在英国威尔斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Gwalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Walthers
meanings: German: patronymic from the personal name Walther (see Walter ).

variants.name: McQuate
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uait ‘son of Uat’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Irish form McQuaid .

variants.name: Waltke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Jurgen, Wilhelmina.
German: from a pet form of any of the ancient Germanic personal names formed with Old High German waltan ‘to rule’ as the first element, as in Walter .

variants.name: McWaters
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaitéir, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of the personal name Walter , with excrescent English -s.

variants.name: Wahler
meanings: German:
from the personal name Wol(d)er, Low German form of Walter .
habitational name for someone from any of various places called Wahl, Wahle, or Wahlen.

variants.name: McWalter
meanings: Irish and Scottish: from Gaelic Mac Uaitéir ‘son of Walter ’.

variants.name: Walters
meanings: English: variant of Walter , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
German: patronymic from   Walter  .

variants.name: McQuaid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Colum, Nuala.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaid ‘son of Uad’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Scottish form McQuate .

variants.name: Walther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Erwin, Wolfgang, Otto, Gerhard, Gunter, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Bernd, Bernhard.
German, Dutch, Swedish, and Danish: variant of   Walter  .

variants.name: Lauter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Erwin, Kurt, Sigismund, Wolf.

German: topographic name and habitational name, from a common stream name meaning ‘clear’, from Middle High German hlutra, or from any of the places called Lauter or Lauta (Saxony). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and some other European countries.
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Lavtar or its Croatized and rare Slovenian variant Lautar: from a dialect variant of the personal name Valter (see Walter ).

nameMeanings.name: Mathew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Matthew .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Mathew ap Gwalter was born about 1235, in Wiston, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Gwgon ap Bleddyn, Arglwydd of Cas wis, was 141 and his mother, Gwenllian verch Philip, was 142. He married Margared Gwys in 1265, in Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.Wiston, Pembroke, Wales: Wiston, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathew ap Gwalter est né environ 1235, à Wiston, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gwgon ap Bleddyn, Arglwydd of Cas wis, avait 141 ans et sa mère, Gwenllian verch Philip, avait 142 ans. Il a épousé Margared Gwys en 1265, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathew ap Gwalter um 1235 in Wiston, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gwgon ap Bleddyn, Arglwydd of Cas wis 141 und seine Mutter Gwenllian verch Philip 142. Er hat 1265 in Wales, United Kingdom Margared Gwys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathew ap Gwalter родился около 1235 г. в местности вистон, пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gwgon ap Bleddyn, Arglwydd of Cas wis было 141 год, а матери, которую звали Gwenllian verch Philip, 142.
